content:
URGENT EXPO GO CRASH AUDIT — CHECK 20 ROOT CAUSES, FIX, TEST, AND PROVE

The IVX Expo/React Native app currently opens this Expo Go screen:

“Something went wrong.
You can go back to Expo home or try to reload the project.”

Do not guess. Open the Expo error log, Metro logs, device logs, and backend logs. Audit all 20 possible root causes below, fix every confirmed issue, then test the real application on Android.

REPOSITORY:
@[github:https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Fibb142%2Frork-global-real-estate-invest:GitHub Sync - ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest]

APP ROOT:
/expo

20 ROOT CAUSES TO AUDIT

1. EXPO SDK MISMATCH
Verify the app SDK matches the currently installed Expo Go version.
Check expo, react-native, react, expo-router, and all Expo packages.

2. INVALID PACKAGE VERSION COMBINATION
Run Expo dependency validation and fix incompatible versions using Expo-compatible package versions only.

3. METRO BUNDLE FAILURE
Start Metro with cache cleared and capture the complete bundling error.
Do not report success unless the Android bundle reaches 100%.

4. STALE METRO OR EXPO CACHE
Clear:
- Metro cache
- Expo cache
- Watchman cache if available
- node_modules cache
- Android Expo Go cached project
Then restart cleanly.

5. WRONG PROJECT ROOT
Confirm Expo is started from:
/expo
Do not start Expo from the repository root if the app package.json is inside /expo.

6. WRONG ENTRYPOINT
Verify:
- package.json main field
- Expo Router entry
- index.js/index.ts if used
- app/_layout.tsx
- app directory structure
Fix any missing or invalid entrypoint.

7. EXPO ROUTER ROUTE CRASH
Audit all initial routes, layouts, redirects, route groups, dynamic routes, and navigation imports.
Confirm the first route renders without throwing.

8. JAVASCRIPT RUNTIME EXCEPTION
Inspect the full device error and stack trace for:
- undefined values
- null property access
- invalid hook usage
- missing exports
- circular dependencies
- startup exceptions

9. MISSING MODULE OR BAD IMPORT
Search for:
- Module not found
- Unable to resolve
- wrong filename capitalization
- incorrect relative path
- missing package
- web-only or server-only imports in mobile code

10. NATIVE MODULE NOT SUPPORTED BY EXPO GO
Identify any package that requires a custom native build and cannot run inside Expo Go.
Either:
- replace it with an Expo Go-compatible package, or
- create an Expo development build
Do not claim Expo Go support if unsupported native modules remain.

11. ENVIRONMENT VARIABLE FAILURE
Verify all startup environment variables are defined and loaded correctly.
The app must not crash if a noncritical variable is missing.
Do not expose secret values.

12. INVALID APP.CONFIG OR APP.JSON
Validate:
- app.json
- app.config.js/ts
- plugins
- scheme
- slug
- Android package
- iOS bundle identifier
- extra configuration
Fix malformed or unsupported configuration.

13. EXPO CONFIG PLUGIN FAILURE
Audit every plugin for:
- wrong version
- wrong options
- unsupported Expo Go behavior
- duplicate plugin
- plugin requiring a development build

14. AUTH INITIALIZATION CRASH
Test startup without owner authentication.
Audit Supabase initialization, auth session restoration, secure storage, token parsing, and redirects.

15. SUPABASE STARTUP FAILURE
Confirm Supabase URL and anon key are available to the mobile runtime.
Add graceful failure handling so a network or auth issue does not crash the entire app.

16. BACKEND/API STARTUP FAILURE
Verify Render API calls are not blocking initial render.
Add timeout, fallback, and safe loading/error states.
The app must still open if the API is temporarily unavailable.

17. SOCKET.IO OR REALTIME STARTUP CRASH
Audit Socket.IO and realtime subscriptions.
Prevent connection errors, invalid URLs, duplicate listeners, or rejected promises from crashing startup.

18. ANDROID-SPECIFIC FAILURE
Audit:
- Android permissions
- unsupported intent
- deep link
- cleartext HTTP
- network security
- keyboard library
- storage permissions
- notification initialization
- device-specific native package behavior

19. MEMORY OR BUNDLE SIZE FAILURE
Check whether startup imports too many screens, assets, videos, images, or large JSON files.
Lazy-load noncritical modules and reduce initial memory usage.

20. CORRUPTED INSTALL OR OLD EXPO GO SESSION
Remove the cached IVX project from Expo Go.
Restart Expo Go.
Generate a fresh QR/tunnel session.
Test on a second Android device or emulator to separate app failure from device cache failure.

MANDATORY EXECUTION

1. Open “View error log” and capture the complete exact error.
2. Run:
   - dependency validation
   - TypeScript check
   - lint
   - Expo diagnostics
   - Metro Android bundle
3. Fix the first real root cause found.
4. Restart from a completely clean cache.
5. Open the application in Expo Go on Android.
6. Confirm the IVX landing screen appears.
7. Test owner sign-in.
8. Open the IVX Owner AI chat.
9. Send a real message and receive an AI response.
10. Close and reopen the app.
11. Confirm the session and chat history persist.
12. Commit and push all fixes to GitHub.
13. Deploy backend changes if required.
14. Repeat the device test after deployment.

REQUIRED PROOF

A. Exact Expo error from “View error log”
B. Exact root cause number from the list above
C. Exact file and line causing the crash
D. Files changed
E. Expo SDK version
F. React Native version
G. Expo Router version
H. Dependency validation result
I. Expo diagnostics result
J. Metro bundle result
K. Android device test result
L. Screenshot of the real IVX app open in Expo Go
M. Owner sign-in result
N. Chat send/response result
O. GitHub commit SHA
P. Active branch
Q. Render deployment ID if backend changed
R. Production /health HTTP result
S. Remaining failures
T. Final status exactly one of:

EXPO_ANDROID_VERIFIED_WORKING

or

EXPO_ANDROID_STILL_BLOCKED_WITH_EXACT_ERROR

Do not answer with general explanations.
Do not tell me only to reload.
Do not mark the app verified from automated tests alone.
Inspect the real Expo error, fix it, run it on Android, and provide visual proof.
